page: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this server allocates shared CPU resources. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates strong compute capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and multi-core performance in the strong tier (top 25%). Passmark CPU physics tests also register in the strong tier. However, single-threaded gzip compression is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests, including cached read-only small block at 3492.82 GB/sec and uncached large block read at 138.44 GB/sec, place the memory subsystem in the average tier (middle 50%). Similarly, Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations at 47.64 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 20.84 GB/sec perform within average parameters.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a memory-optimized option. The shared CPU allocation introduces a potential tradeoff in environments requiring sustained, dedicated CPU performance, though the physical core count mitigates some multi-threaded bottlenecks. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and moderate web serving operations that benefit from a large memory footprint and local NVMe storage without requiring dedicated CPU guarantees.
description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with shared CPU allocation, it provides 256.0 GB of memory (8.0 GB per core) and a bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core and strong multi-core CPU performance, though single-threaded gzip compression is weak. Memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving throughput align with average tier standards. Lacking a GPU, this instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing where a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage are required, balancing high memory capacity against shared CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd is an AMD EPYC Milan server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1600 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 1600 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring thirty-two AMD EPYC cores, a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio, and local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Milan processor with 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
Bundled 1600 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Suited for database hosting, memory-intensive applications, and multi-threaded processing
